question: [File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: setIsAuthorized -> Midnight.multicall + repay + callback loop] Can an unprivileged authorized operator compose a multicall with N repay calls each with a callback contract (IRepayCallback.onRepay), causing N external callback calls plus N storage writes, and can an expensive onRepay implementation compound this into a block-level DoS? Preconditions: operator is authorized; borrower has debt >= N; callback has expensive onRepay logic. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) multicall([repay(market, 1, borrower, expensiveCallback, '') x N]). Invariant tested: repay + callback must not compound into unbounded gas via multicall. Scoped impact: gas griefing. Proof idea: fuzz callback gas cost and N, measure total gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: setIsAuthorized -> Midnight.touchMarket + isLltvAllowed check] Can an unprivileged market creator call touchMarket with 128 collaterals where each collateral requires the isLltvAllowed check (9 equality comparisons per collateral), causing 128*9 = 1152 comparisons in a single touchMarket call, and compose M such calls in a multicall to cause O(M*1152) comparisons? Preconditions: M distinct valid 128-collateral markets; multicall is unbounded. Call sequence: multicall([touchMarket(128 collaterals) x M]). Invariant tested: LLTV validation loop must not be composable into block-level DoS. Scoped impact: gas griefing. Proof idea: fuzz M, measure comparison overhead.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: setIsAuthorized -> Midnight.multicall + liquidate + callback] Can an unprivileged liquidator compose a multicall with N liquidate calls each with an ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate callback, where each call triggers the 16-oracle collateralBitmap loop plus an expensive callback, causing O(N*(16*oracle_gas + callback_gas)) total gas and exhausting block gas? Preconditions: borrower has 16 activated collaterals; position is liquidatable; callback has expensive onLiquidate logic. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) multicall([liquidate(callback=expensiveCallback) x N]). Invariant tested: liquidate + callback must not compound into unbounded gas via multicall. Scoped impact: gas griefing. Proof idea: fuzz callback gas cost and N, measure total gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: setIsAuthorized -> Midnight.multicall + take + consumed mapping writes] Can an unprivileged taker compose a multicall with N take calls on offers sharing the same consumption group, where each take writes to consumed[maker][group] (storage write) plus triggers isHealthy (16 oracle calls), causing O(N*(storage + 16*oracle)) gas, and does the consumed mapping write add meaningful overhead per call? Preconditions: N valid offers share the same group; seller has 16 activated collaterals; taker is authorized. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) multicall([take(offer_i, same group) x N]). Invariant tested: consumed mapping writes + isHealthy must not compound into unbounded gas. Scoped impact: gas griefing. Proof idea: fuzz N, measure storage write overhead.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: setIsAuthorized -> Midnight.multicall + supplyCollateral + countBits] Can an unprivileged authorized operator compose a multicall with N supplyCollateral calls each activating a new collateral bit (triggering countBits check at line 539), where countBits is O(1) but the storage write to collateralBitmap and collateral[index] adds overhead, and compose this with isHealthy calls to cause O(N*(storage + 16*oracle)) gas? Preconditions: operator is authorized; borrower has < 16 activated collaterals; market has 16+ collaterals. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) multicall([supplyCollateral(i, 1) x 16, isHealthy x N]). Invariant tested: supplyCollateral + isHealthy must not compound into unbounded gas. Scoped impact: gas griefing. Proof idea: fuzz N, measure gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: setIsAuthorized -> Midnight.multicall + take + _updatePosition + lossFactor mulDiv] Can an unprivileged taker compose a multicall with N take calls where each take triggers _updatePosition for buyer and seller (each computing mulDivDown(credit, type(uint128).max - lossFactor, type(uint128).max - lastLossFactor)), and does a high lossFactor value cause the mulDiv computation to be more expensive, compounding gas cost across N calls? Preconditions: market has high lossFactor; buyer and seller have credit; N valid offers exist. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) multicall([take(offer_i) x N]). Invariant tested: _updatePosition mulDiv must not cause variable gas cost that enables griefing. Scoped impact: gas griefing. Proof idea: fuzz lossFactor and N, measure gas variance.
